evals.prompt: I'm building a habit tracker iOS app. Build the main screen in SwiftUI where users see today's habits and check them off. Make it look really good.
evals.expected_output: Research + design brief with rationale, design tokens, a SwiftUI view using Dynamic Type/semantic colors/springs with all states, and a scored 55-item checklist report
assertions: No fixed font sizes: all text uses Dynamic Type styles or .custom(relativeTo:)
Reduce Motion accessibility setting is read and honored
No decorative gradient identity (gradient icon tiles, gradient accents)
Designed empty state exists: states what's missing, why, and gives the filling action
Design rationale documented with named real competitors and rejected alternatives
Color tokens defined with verified contrast in both light and dark mode

evals.prompt: Create a landing page for my invoicing app for freelancers, it's called Brightbill. Single self-contained HTML file. Make it look professional, not AI-generated.
evals.expected_output: Research + design brief with tokens, a self-contained landing page free of AI-slop tells with real states and verified contrast, and a scored 43-item checklist report
assertions: No banned reflex fonts (Inter, Roboto, Space Grotesk, Geist) as product typography
No eyebrow pill chips, badge chips, or fake-metric stats band
Email/CTA form includes designed error, loading, and success states
Named competitor research and rejected alternatives documented
